entry.title: Truro Cathedral
entry.content:
The recital will begin at 1:10pm. Please see the cathedral website here for more details, including a livestream link.

Programme

Prelude in E minor “the Great”
Nicolaus Bruhns (d. 1697)

Four Extemporisations
Percy Whitlock (1903-1946)

  1. Divertimento
  2. Fidelis
  3. Fanfare

Penguins’ Playtime
Nigel Ogden (1954-2026)

Passacaglia on a theme of Dunstable
John Weaver (1937-2021)
entry.summary: The recital will begin at 1:10pm. Please see the cathedral website here for more details, including a livestream link. Programme

entry.title: Hampstead Parish Church
entry.content:
Programme

Passacaglia on a theme of Dunstable
John Weaver (1937–2021)

Chaconne in C minor
Dieterich Buxtehude (1637–1707)

Fantasia in A minor
William Byrd (d. 1623)

Four Extemporisations
Percy Whitlock (1903–1946)

  1. Fidelis
  2. Fanfare

Alleluias
Simon Preston (1938–2022)

entry.title: Queens’ College, Cambridge
entry.content:
An all-English programme on the beautiful Binns organ. You can read more about the instrument here.

Programme

I: Allegro Maestoso from Sonata in G major, Op. 28
Edward Elgar (1857–1934)

Four Extemporisations
Percy Whitlock (1903–1946)

  1. Fidelis
  2. Fanfare

Fantasia in F
Peter Philips (1561–1628)

Fantasia in A minor
William Byrd (d. 1623)

Adagio in E
Frank Bridge (1879–1941)
entry.summary: An all-English programme on the beautiful Binns organ. You can read more about the instrument here. Programme

entry.title: Keble College, Oxford
entry.content:
The recital begins at 20:30, preceding Compline at 21:00. You can rewatch it here.

Programme

Præludium in G major
Nicolaus Bruhns (d. 1697)

Adagio
Nicolaus Bruhns (?) (d. 1697)

Litanies
Jehan Alain (1911–1940)

Cortège et Litanie
Marcel Dupré (1886–1971)

2nd Verset on Conditor Alme Siderum
Jean Titelouze (c. 1563–1633)
entry.summary: The recital begins at 20:30, preceding Compline at 21:00. You can rewatch it here. Programme

entry.title: Magdalen College, Oxford
entry.content:
Programme

Prelude in G major
Nicolaus Bruhns (d. 1697)

Vater Unser in Himmelreich
Heinrich Scheidemann (ca. 1596–1663)

  1. Vater Unser in Himmelreich

J. S. Bach (1685–1750)

  1. Vater Unser in Himmelreich – BWV 682
  2. Vater Unser in Himmelreich – BWV 683

Alleluias
Simon Preston (1938–2022)

Litanies
Jehan Alain (1911–1940)

Cortège et Litanie
Marcel Dupré (1886–1971)

entry.title: St Bride’s, Fleet Street
entry.content:
Programme

March “Dignity and Impudence”
Percy Whitlock (1903–1946), arr. Malcolm Riley

“Gigue” Fugue in G major, BWV 577
Johann Sebastian Bach (1685–1750)

Praeludium in G major
Nicolaus Bruhns (d. 1697)

“Gigue” Fugue in C major, BuxWV 174
Dieterich Buxtehude (1637–1707)

Marche héroïque
Herbert Brewer (1865–1928)

entry.title: Merton College, Oxford
entry.content:
Programme

I: Allegro Maestoso from Organ Sonata in G major
Edward Elgar (1857–1934)

Toccata duodecima et ultima
Georg Muffat (1653–1704)

Dic Nobis Maria (intabulation after Bassano)
Heinrich Scheidemann (1595–1663)

Vater unser in Himmelreich (BWV 682)
Johann Sebastian Bach (1685–1750)

VII. Final from Sept Pièces
Marcel Dupré (1886–1971)

entry.title: Canterbury Cathedral
entry.content:
Programme

I: Allegro Maestoso from Organ Sonata in G major
Edward Elgar (1857–1934)

Tiento de secondo tono por gesolreut sobre la Letanía de la Virgen
Pablo Bruna (1611–1679)

Dic Nobis Maria (intabulation after Bassano)
Heinrich Scheidemann (1595–1663)

Prelude in E minor “the Great”
Nicolaus Bruhns (d. 1697)

Three Preludes on French Hymns
Derek Healey (b. 1936)

  • I. Christe Sanctorum
  • II. Picardy
  • III. O Quanta Qualia

Excerpts from Sept Pièces
Marcel Dupré (1886–1971)

  • II. Souvenir
  • VII. Final

entry.title: St Michael and All Angels’, Croydon

link.title: St Michael and All Angels’, Croydon

entry.content:
Programme

I: Allegro Maestoso from Sonata in G major, op. 28
Edward Elgar (1857-1934)

A Fancy
William Byrd (d. 1623)

II: Adagio (from Three Pieces for Organ)
Frank Bridge (1879-1941)

Fugue in G major “alla Gigue” (BWV 577)
J. S. Bach (1685-1750)

Excerpts from Sept Pièces
Marcel Dupré (1886-1971)

  • I. Souvenir
  • VII. Final
